skill.name: incident-command-governor
skill.description: Build governed incident command packets from operational notes, severity evidence, owners, timelines, and stakeholder communication needs.
skill.summary: Create structured incident packets with severity, ownership, communications, and review notes.
skill.author: yaojingang

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported weak crypto and shell execution patterns, but review found false positives. The flagged lines are incident-command wording and Markdown inline code references, with no confirmed malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Crypto Pattern From Incident Command Text
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography on lines that describe incident command workflows. These lines do not reference cryptographic algorithms, hashes, ciphers, secrets, or credential handling.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is plain product description using the word command. No cryptographic primitive or security-sensitive operation is present on those lines.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Inline Code Flagged As Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis treated Markdown backticks as Ruby or shell backtick execution. The referenced lines contain inline-code labels for severity names, files, and a date.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The backticks are Markdown formatting in documentation. They do not appear in Ruby code, shell code, or an executable command context.

content.value_statement: Incident notes often lack clear severity, ownership, and audience-specific communication. This skill turns operational evidence into a governed packet for response, review, and follow-up.
actual_capabilities: Structures incident notes into a standard command packet.
Uses a severity matrix to assign or confirm severity.
Separates internal, leadership, and external communication needs.
Captures owners, next actions, timelines, and affected scope.
Includes governance notes for monthly review and maintenance.
limitations: It does not replace live incident commander judgment.
It needs accurate incident evidence to assign severity.
It does not send stakeholder messages automatically.
It does not integrate with paging, ticketing, or monitoring systems.

use_cases.title: Package Active Incident Notes
use_cases.description: Convert responder notes into a packet with timeline, impact, severity, owners, and communications.

use_cases.title: Standardize Review Artifacts
use_cases.description: Prepare consistent evidence and governance notes before an incident review meeting.

use_cases.title: Align Stakeholder Updates
use_cases.description: Separate responder, leadership, and external updates while keeping severity and impact consistent.

prompt_templates.title: Create A Basic Packet
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner use with clear incident notes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to build an incident command packet from these notes. Include summary, severity, timeline, impact, owners, and stakeholder updates.

prompt_templates.title: Assess Severity
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate use when severity is unclear.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these incident details against the severity matrix. State the severity, evidence, uncertainty, and any missing information before drafting the packet.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Communications
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a governed incident packet and separate communication blocks for responders, leadership, and external stakeholders. Keep external wording scoped and simple.

prompt_templates.title: Govern A Review Workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced use for repeatable operations governance.
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a complete incident command packet with severity rationale, ownership, open risks, communication blocks, and governance notes for monthly review.

output_examples.input: A customer-facing dashboard outage lasted 14 minutes after a rollout and required rollback.
output_examples.output: The output identifies a high-severity customer impact, lists rollout and rollback timeline events, assigns action owners, and drafts separate stakeholder updates.

output_examples.input: Responder notes include degraded alerts, unclear customer impact, and no confirmed owner.
output_examples.output: The output flags missing evidence, avoids unsupported severity downgrades, and asks for ownership before finalizing the packet.

output_examples.input: Leadership needs an incident summary, but responders also need detailed mitigation notes.
output_examples.output: The output separates leadership impact language from responder detail while keeping timeline, severity, and next checkpoint aligned.

best_practices: Provide timeline evidence, affected scope, and severity signals together.
Name owners for every action before finalizing the packet.
Review severity and communication policy after major process changes.
anti_patterns: Do not use it for a single short status update only.
Do not guess severity when evidence is incomplete.
Do not reuse internal responder language for external audiences.

faq.question: What does this skill produce?
faq.answer: It produces a structured incident command packet with severity, timeline, impact, owners, communications, and governance notes.

faq.question: Can it determine severity automatically?
faq.answer: It can assess severity from provided evidence and the included matrix. It reports uncertainty when evidence is missing.

faq.question: Does it send incident updates?
faq.answer: No. It drafts communication blocks for different audiences, but it does not send messages or call external services.

faq.question: What inputs work best?
faq.answer: Use incident summaries, timeline events, affected systems, customer impact, severity signals, owners, and stakeholder audiences.

faq.question: Is it only for active incidents?
faq.answer: No. It can also prepare review packets after an incident if the evidence is available.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
